betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The pride of the superior.
Or rather, the pride of the one who sees themselves as such.
It is not just a simple feeling of superiority, though. If it were just that, proofs of the contrary would eventually pile up.
It is not just narcissism. Superiority does not come innately from the self.
It is a more insidious feeling.
The superiority, it comes from the Mission. A mission of order, of virtue, of leadership. 
The knowledge of a simple and yet dangerous truth. That you, as you are now, know for sure to be an agent of good.
And since you are an agent of good, well, maybe you are better than everyone else. Maybe your desires and objectives must indeed come before others'.
The yoke of the Master of Order, the yoke of the Arbiter.
It too, I see in my reflection in the Spren, is part of the load I now bear.
This struggle, it is mine too. The knowledge of virtue.
That by turning my back to the projects of the Meatball Man, by coming back as an Undead Lord, I am reinstating virtue and goodness in the universe.
But no, the good, it does not come from me. The good, the right, the just, they exist outside of me. It are... they just are.
I am just given a life, a fate, a time to express my freedom, and to affirm companionship with this good, or to refuse its embrace, every day, every hour. 
Being good is not something that can come from within. It is a recognition of good in others, that in turn brings us to embrace goodness ourselves. Because, well, a good world is a better world.
For everyone.
